messages.content:
CVE-2020-35727 refers to a reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability identified in Quest Policy Authority version 8.1.2.200. This flaw allows remote attackers to potentially inject malicious scripts into the browser by exploiting the title parameter in a crafted link to the BrowseDirs.do file.

The vulnerability is categorized under CWE-79, indicating improper neutralization of user input before output generation in web pages. Quest Policy Authority for Unified Communications, version 8.1.2.200, is impacted, with the issue residing in unsupported products by the vendor.

Rated with a MEDIUM severity, the potential impact of this vulnerability includes unauthorized execution of scripts within the context of a user's session, exposing sensitive user data. Mitigation focuses on input validation, sanitation procedures (M1050, M1051), applying security patches and updates (M1048, M1021), and minimizing attack surfaces by reviewing exposed services (M1017).

Furthermore, this vulnerability correlates with the MITRE ATT&CK technique T1189, associated with drive-by compromise tactics.
